theme: Fold a chain on a lattice without self-trapping
title: Resonant Folds: Binding an H/P Chain Without Self-Trapping
strategies: trivial: hairpin (2-row serpentine) fold; reproduces the checker baseline
greedy: coarse width search (small widths, sqrt(L), hairpin) keeping the best-scoring compact fold
strong: fine sweep over every serpentine width, exploiting the checkerboard parity invariant to find the hidden resonant width where opposite-parity binding residues stack into contacts

insight: only opposite-parity (odd chain-distance) residues can bind; the score-maximising fold width is a non-round value the coarse search skips, and choosing it stops late residues from being stranded
